name: Disability Tax Credit application: marked restriction pathway

estimated_dollars.disclaimer: Approximate annual federal plus possible provincial value. TaxBridge cannot determine eligibility; only CRA can approve a DTC application.

documents_needed: Form T2201 Disability Tax Credit Certificate
Examples of how the restriction affects daily functioning
filing_destination: Apply for the DTC through CRA My Account or Form T2201 before claiming it on a tax return
fallback_explanation: Your answers suggest it may be worth applying for the DTC under the marked restriction pathway. The CRA, not TaxBridge, decides eligibility after reviewing Form T2201 certified by a qualified medical practitioner.
maintenance_note: DTC amounts and provincial supplements should be verified annually.

name: Disability Tax Credit application: cumulative effect pathway

estimated_dollars.disclaimer: Approximate annual federal plus possible provincial value. This is an application screen, not an eligibility decision.

documents_needed: Form T2201 Disability Tax Credit Certificate
Notes on the combined effect of restrictions across daily activities
filing_destination: Apply for the DTC through CRA My Account or Form T2201 before claiming it on a tax return
fallback_explanation: Your answers suggest it may be worth applying under the cumulative effect pathway, where two or more significant restrictions combine to create an effect similar to one marked restriction. CRA approval still depends on the certified Form T2201.
maintenance_note: DTC amounts and provincial supplements should be verified annually.

name: Disability Tax Credit application: life-sustaining therapy pathway

estimated_dollars.disclaimer: Approximate annual federal plus possible provincial value. CRA must approve Form T2201 before this can be claimed.

documents_needed: Form T2201 Disability Tax Credit Certificate
Medical practitioner certification of life-sustaining therapy
Records showing therapy time and frequency
filing_destination: Apply for the DTC through CRA My Account or Form T2201 before claiming it on a tax return
fallback_explanation: Your answers suggest it may be worth applying under the life-sustaining therapy pathway. CRA rules include specific time and frequency tests, so bring clear therapy records to the practitioner completing Form T2201.
maintenance_note: DTC life-sustaining therapy criteria should be checked if rules change.

name: Potential retroactive DTC refund

estimated_dollars.disclaimer: This is only useful if you or a supporting family member had federal tax payable in those years. Actual refunds vary widely.

documents_needed: Notice of Assessment for each year you want reassessed
CRA My Account access or Form T1-ADJ
filing_destination: After T2201 approval, request reassessment through CRA My Account or Form T1-ADJ
fallback_explanation: If your DTC application is approved, CRA may reassess up to 10 previous tax years. Whether that creates a refund depends on whether you or a supporting family member had tax payable in those years, so this should be treated as potential, not guaranteed money.
maintenance_note: Per-year value estimate based on Alberta adult examples; verify annually.

name: DTC child supplement

estimated_dollars.disclaimer: Maximum supplement estimate; actual value depends on tax payable and family circumstances.

documents_needed: Approved Form T2201 for the child
Parent or guardian tax return information
filing_destination: DTC claim for a child under 18 after CRA approves Form T2201
fallback_explanation: If a child under 18 is approved for the DTC, the family may be able to claim the DTC child supplement. This is separate from the Child Disability Benefit and depends on CRA approval and the family tax situation.
maintenance_note: Child-related benefit amounts are indexed and should be verified annually.

name: Child Disability Benefit

estimated_dollars.disclaimer: Current maximum for July 2025 to June 2026 per CRA; actual amount depends on family net income.

documents_needed: Approved Form T2201 for the child
Filed tax returns for the parent or guardian
filing_destination: CRA administers the Child Disability Benefit through the Canada Child Benefit system after DTC approval
fallback_explanation: If your child is approved for the DTC, your family may also receive the Child Disability Benefit through the Canada Child Benefit system. The amount changes by benefit year and depends on family income.
maintenance_note: Verify Child Disability Benefit amounts each benefit year.

name: Transfer disability amount to a supporting family member

estimated_dollars.disclaimer: Potential value depends on the supporting family member's tax payable and province.

filing_destination: Line 31800 for disability amount transferred from a dependant, if applicable
fallback_explanation: If you are approved for the DTC but cannot use the full amount yourself, a supporting family member may be able to claim a transferred disability amount. This depends on the relationship, support facts, and their tax payable.

name: Registered Disability Savings Plan unlock

estimated_dollars.disclaimer: Annual grant and bond potential; lifetime government contributions can be much higher. Actual amount depends on contributions, income, and RDSP rules.

documents_needed: Bank or financial institution RDSP application requirements
filing_destination: Open an RDSP through a participating financial institution after DTC approval
fallback_explanation: DTC approval is required to open an RDSP. The RDSP can unlock government grants and bonds, and in Alberta RDSP funds are generally exempt from AISH income calculations.
maintenance_note: RDSP grant and bond amounts should be verified annually.

name: Possible Canada Workers Benefit disability supplement

estimated_dollars.disclaimer: Possibly eligible only. CRA income, province, family-status, and full-time-student exclusion rules must be checked.

filing_destination: Canada Workers Benefit section of the T1 return, if CRA criteria are met
fallback_explanation: Because you had work income, the Canada Workers Benefit disability supplement may be worth checking after DTC approval. This is not automatic: CRA applies income, province, family-status, and full-time-student exclusion rules.
maintenance_note: CWB amounts and thresholds change and should be verified annually.

name: Find a qualified practitioner first

estimated_dollars.disclaimer: Procedural step. A practitioner must certify Form T2201 before CRA can decide.

documents_needed: List of current treatments, medications, and assistive devices
Contact information for a doctor, nurse practitioner, or other eligible certifier
filing_destination: Start Part A through CRA My Account or by phone, then have a practitioner complete Part B
fallback_explanation: The biggest next step is finding a qualified practitioner who can certify the right section of Form T2201. Nurse practitioners can certify all sections, and disability advocacy organizations may be able to help you prepare.

name: Consider re-screening after federal DTC rule changes

estimated_dollars.disclaimer: Reapplication guidance only. CRA still decides based on current certification and review.

documents_needed: Previous DTC denial letter if available
filing_destination: Review current CRA DTC eligibility rules and consider a new Form T2201 application
fallback_explanation: Because your denial was before 2022, it may be worth re-screening under updated DTC criteria, especially for mental functions or life-sustaining therapy. This does not mean approval is guaranteed; CRA still relies on current medical certification.

name: AISH DTC reapplication requirement
documents_needed: Previous DTC denial letter if available
filing_destination: Follow Alberta guidance for AISH recipients applying for federal disability supports
fallback_explanation: Alberta states that AISH recipients denied DTC eligibility before 2025 are required to reapply because federal eligibility criteria changed. This is separate from whether CRA ultimately approves the DTC application.
maintenance_note: Alberta AISH policy should be rechecked if deadlines change.

name: AISH and Canada Disability Benefit warning

estimated_dollars.disclaimer: Alberta-specific informational warning. Do not present the adult CDB as additive monthly income for AISH recipients.

documents_needed: Canada Disability Benefit application status if applicable
filing_destination: Review Alberta's AISH federal disability supports guidance
fallback_explanation: For AISH recipients: Alberta requires you to apply for federal disability supports including the Canada Disability Benefit and DTC pathway. Per Alberta government policy, if your CDB application decision has not been made by February 28, 2026, $200 in CDB income will be reduced from your AISH benefits starting April 2026. The CDB is treated as exempt income only for AISH recipients eligible for the Modified Living Allowance. Applying for the DTC may still help with retroactive tax refunds and RDSP access, but TaxBridge cannot promise your monthly income will increase.
maintenance_note: Uses Alberta wording and dates from the v5 source; verify before future use.
